project: MatFlow — Sprint 5: Error prevention + DB hardening
scope: 9 stories across 2 tiers. Tier 1 = critical error visibility + revenue safety + tenant-scope sweep + spam protection + dependency audit. Tier 2 = DB integrity (CHECK constraints, optimistic concurrency, soft-delete/indexes). 3 staged migrations (NOT auto-applied). Pinned baseline tests must drop to 0.

stories.title: Tier 1.1: Fix 4 pinned baseline test failures
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/checkin-duplicate.test.ts: both failing tests pass — fix mocks for US-009 tenantId addition (instance lookup needs to include tenant.id in mock returns)
tests/integration/security.test.ts F3: both failing tests pass — fix mocks for US-012 401-not-404 change (auth gate now returns 401 not 404 for unauthenticated checkin)
npm test reports 0 failures (was 4 baseline)
No production code changes — test mocks only
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 1.2: Env-gate visibility — informative 503 on missing env vars
acceptanceCriteria: Apply the BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN visibility pattern (e6a3ad8) to all env-dependent routes
/api/auth/forgot-password returns 503 with 'Email service not configured. Set RESEND_API_KEY.' when RESEND_API_KEY is unset (production fail-closed; dev fallback logs the OTP)
/api/cron/monthly-reports returns 503 with 'AI service not configured. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.' when key is unset (currently no-ops silently)
/api/drive/connect returns 503 with 'Google Drive not configured. Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID + GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.' when either is unset
Verify magic-link/request already does this (it does — keep as canonical example)
Vitest test asserting 503 + clear error body for at least one of the new gates
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 1.3: 5 missing Stripe webhook handlers
acceptanceCriteria: /api/stripe/webhook handles customer.subscription.updated — updates Member.stripeSubscriptionId + paymentStatus
Handles invoice.voided — flips Payment.status from succeeded to refunded if matched
Handles payment_intent.succeeded — same idempotency pattern as invoice.payment_succeeded (StripeEvent unique check)
Handles customer.deleted — sets Member.stripeCustomerId=null on matching members
Handles payment_method.detached — log to AuditLog only (no Member field today)
All handlers tenant-scoped via stripe metadata.tenantId where present, OR via Member.stripeCustomerId lookup
All handlers idempotent via StripeEvent.eventId unique constraint (already in schema)
Vitest test for at least subscription.updated + invoice.voided handlers
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 1.4: Defensive tenant-scope ESLint rule + sweep
acceptanceCriteria: Custom ESLint rule at eslint-rules/no-tenant-unsafe-prisma.js: flags prisma.{Member|Class|MembershipTier|...}.findUnique without a tenantId filter; flags update/delete on tenant-scoped models without tenantId in WHERE
Rule wired into eslint.config (or .eslintrc) — fails CI when violated
Sweep all violations the rule finds — replace findUnique with findFirst + tenantId, or add explicit allow-comment with justification (e.g. webhook lookups by stripe IDs which are globally unique)
Document the rule + suppress-comment policy in eslint-rules/README.md
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 1.5: /apply spam protection (rate limit)
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/apply rate-limited via existing lib/rate-limit.ts: 5 attempts per hour per IP (returns 429 + Retry-After when exceeded)
Bucket key: apply:{ip} (IP from existing getClientIp helper)
Existing form behaviour unchanged when under the limit
Vitest test for the rate limit
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 1.6: npm audit baseline + fix CRITICAL/HIGH
acceptanceCriteria: Run npm audit; record baseline at docs/NPM-AUDIT-BASELINE.md
All CRITICAL CVEs fixed (npm audit fix or manual upgrade)
All HIGH CVEs fixed OR explicitly waived in the doc with justification
npm run build still passes after upgrades
npm test still passes after upgrades

stories.title: Tier 2.1: Schema CHECK constraints for enum-like fields
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 20260430000001_schema_check_constraints staged (NOT auto-applied)
ADD CONSTRAINT for Member.accountType IN ('adult', 'junior', 'kids')
ADD CONSTRAINT for Member.status IN ('active', 'inactive', 'cancelled', 'taster')
ADD CONSTRAINT for Member.paymentStatus IN ('paid', 'overdue', 'paused', 'free', 'pending', 'cancelled')
ADD CONSTRAINT for User.role IN ('owner', 'manager', 'coach', 'admin')
ADD CONSTRAINT for Payment.status IN ('succeeded', 'failed', 'refunded', 'disputed', 'pending')
ADD CONSTRAINT for MembershipTier.billingCycle IN ('monthly', 'annual', 'none')
Schema.prisma comments document the allowed values next to each field
Migration is non-destructive — runs CHECK NOT VALID then VALIDATE so existing rows aren't blocked even if they drift
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 2.2: Optimistic concurrency on PATCH endpoints
acceptanceCriteria: /api/members/[id] PATCH accepts X-If-Unmodified-Since header (or body.updatedAt). Compares to row's current updatedAt; returns 409 with current value if mismatch
/api/staff/[id] PATCH same pattern
Dashboard list views (MembersList, staff page) trigger SWR revalidation on 409 + show 'Reload — another user updated this row' toast
Vitest test for the 409 path on both endpoints
npm run build passes

stories.title: Tier 2.3: Soft-delete + index audit
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 20260430000002_soft_delete_extensions staged: RankSystem.deletedAt + Class.deletedAt (already partially via isActive but make explicit)
All findMany queries on RankSystem/Class default-filter where: { deletedAt: null }
Index audit: scan @@index directives and confirm each matches an actual query pattern; remove orphans
Migration 20260430000003_index_cleanup staged for any drops/adds
Document at docs/SCHEMA-INDEX-MAP.md — each index linked to the route(s) that use it
npm run build passes

stories.title: Final verification + push
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build clean
npm test reports 0 failures (the 4 baseline must drop to 0 after US-501)
Per-batch commits pushed to origin/main
3 staged migrations 20260430000001/000002/000003 present, NOT auto-applied
Architect THOROUGH verification approved (security/architectural changes — Stripe handlers, tenant-scope sweep, schema constraints)
All previous US-501..US-509 have passes:true
